title: chenopodium ambrosioides var. genuinum
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides var. genuinum, also known as Mexican tea or epazote, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a carminative, anthelmintic, and antispasmodic, making it effective in relieving digestive issues, parasitic infections, and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Chenopodium ambrosioides var. genuinum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms associated with menstrual disorders, such as heavy bleeding and nausea. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be infused as a tea, dried and smoked as an herbal cigarette, or used as a tincture to reap its medicinal benefits.
